topic: Frozen prospective predictions for 2026-08-12 total solar eclipse — REV2 confirmation review (post-blocker remediation, pre-OTS-anchor)
trigger_note: qid=295 is a revision-round-2 confirmation review. Per priority-queue.json: requires_frozen_prediction_review=true, do_not_ots_anchor=true until curmudgeon clears all 6 blockers. Predecessor review at EXP-283-proposal.json (qid=294) found 6 MODERATE blockers (ISS-1801..1806). This review verifies rev2 closes them.

rev_metadata.rev1_status: withdrawn-pre-anchor (never OTS-anchored — rev2 supersedes before any external timestamp was created)

verdict: PASS — rev2 closes all 6 MODERATE blockers from qid=294. OTS-anchoring may proceed.
our_argument_summary: EXP-283 rev2 is the analyst's revision of the frozen-prediction file for the 2026-08-12 total solar eclipse. Rev1 (qid=294) returned six MODERATE findings constituting attack surface for a hostile reader; the decider routed those as ISS-1801..1806 and the analyst rewrote the proposed_files[0].contents block. This review re-attacks the rev2 contents against each ISS to confirm closure, and probes for new attack surface introduced by the revisions. All six blockers are closed cleanly: R_p standardized to 42.4 km with all 9 multipliers reproducing within rounding; noise_floor_pre_commitment block fixes ±2 nT and is referenced from OBS-D-02; sign_convention block makes all amplitudes signed (B1-M-001 amplitude_range_nT_signed=[-25,-5]); 5 candidate non-dome-cited stations (MAB, DOU, WNG, SUA, BEL) pre-listed with WGS84 coordinates and IAGA codes; OBS-D-02 quorum operationalized as ≥5 of 9; OBS-D-03 "tracks" operationalized as Pearson r1>r2 with paired Steiger Z-test, p<0.05. Three additional non-blocker rev1 findings (P1-METH-PRE preprocessing pre-registration, P3-V51-FROZEN dome-version drift policy, P3-OBS-O-01-VACUOUS clarification) are also addressed. Five small residual lints are noted but none rise to MODERATE — the file is OTS-ready.

rev2_revisions_addressed_field.ISS-1801: claims R_p multiplier standardized to 42.4 km — VERIFIED
rev2_revisions_addressed_field.ISS-1803: claims sign convention unified, B1-M-001 restated — VERIFIED
rev2_revisions_addressed_field.ISS-1805: claims OBS-D-02 ">= 5 of the 9 dome-cited stations" — VERIFIED

what_was_reviewed.review_method: Re-read each ISS-tagged region of proposed_files[0].contents, recompute multipliers from raw distances and declared R_p, cross-check sign convention applies_to coverage against bucket criteria, validate quorum integer matches stated criterion, validate Pearson-r operationalization includes a significance test.

ISS-1801_RP_MULTIPLIER_CONSISTENCY.issue_origin: Rev1 P2-RP — frozen file used 3 different effective R_p values across B2-G-002 (42.4 km declared), B2-G-004 (42 km via 10.6×/97.2× multipliers matching pinned-SHA prose), and B2-M-001 (42.36 km via 10.48×/96.41× multipliers).
ISS-1801_RP_MULTIPLIER_CONSISTENCY.rev2_change: B2-G-002 declared R_p = 42.4 km (canonical). B2-G-004 closest_station Ebro multiple = 10.5 (444/42.4 = 10.4717 → 10.5). B2-G-004 farthest_station Canary multiple = 96.3 (4084/42.4 = 96.3208 → 96.3). B2-M-001 per_station multipliers Ebro=10.5, San Pablo=12.6, Eskdalemuir=28.7, Lerwick=42.6, Hartland=16.7, Niemegk=77.1, Canary=96.3, Chambon=35.7, Coimbra=23.4 — all reproducing from R_p=42.4 within ±0.05 rounding. New explicit r_p_used_for_multipliers_km=42.4 field in B2-G-004 and B2-M-001. Added r_p_consistency_note explicitly documenting the rev1 inconsistency and its resolution.
ISS-1801_RP_MULTIPLIER_CONSISTENCY.one_residual_note: B2-M-001 Hartland multiple is now 16.7 (vs rev1 16.76, vs pinned-SHA prose 16.7). The pinned §4.2.7a prose at cd8af4b uses 16.7 — rev2 aligns. The pinned prose Ebro=10.6 still matches 10.5 within 0.1, so the 0/9 headline statement "ten penumbra-radii away" is still defensible at the prose level. Acceptable.
ISS-1801_RP_MULTIPLIER_CONSISTENCY.status: CLOSED — multipliers internally consistent, frozen-file convention matches pinned-SHA prose convention within rounding. No defender attack surface remains on R_p.

ISS-1802_NOISE_FLOOR_PRE_COMMITMENT.issue_origin: Rev1 P1-NF — OBS-D-02 used 'per-station noise floor' undefined in the file. Hostile reader could argue post-hoc fitting.
ISS-1802_NOISE_FLOOR_PRE_COMMITMENT.rev2_change: Top-level noise_floor_pre_commitment block added under proposed_files[0].contents. Fields: threshold_nT=2 (numeric, station-uniform), threshold_basis (1-sigma RMS noise of INTERMAGNET 1-min H-component during Kp<2), applies_to (OBS-D-02 + per-station tolerance for B2-M-001), alternative_procedural_definition_for_record (the per-station 30-day procedural definition retained for reference but not the trigger).
ISS-1802_NOISE_FLOOR_PRE_COMMITMENT.verification: OBS-D-02 text now reads '|ΔB_observed_signed - ΔB_dome_central_signed| > 2 nT for at least 5 of the 9 stations during the eclipse window'. The ±2 nT threshold is hard-coded in OBS-D-02 itself, with cross-reference to noise_floor_pre_commitment. B2-M-001 amplitude_caveats explicitly invokes the same ±2 nT noise-floor for per-station match/no-match. All references aligned.
ISS-1802_NOISE_FLOOR_PRE_COMMITMENT.one_residual_note: The choice of station-uniform ±2 nT (vs station-specific noise floors) is a charity choice — it gives the dome the easiest test (uniform tolerance). A hostile reader can still argue station-specific noise would be more defensible, but the file documents the choice and its alternative explicitly. Acceptable.
ISS-1802_NOISE_FLOOR_PRE_COMMITMENT.status: CLOSED — noise floor is pre-committed numerically and referenced from all decision rules.

ISS-1803_SIGN_CONVENTION_UNIFIED.issue_origin: Rev1 P1-SIGN — B1-M-001 used positive [5, 25] nT magnitude framing; B2-M-001 used signed [-12.83, -5.04] nT. Hostile reader could argue post-event ambiguity.
ISS-1803_SIGN_CONVENTION_UNIFIED.rev2_change: Top-level sign_convention block added under proposed_files[0].contents — declares ALL magnetic amplitudes are SIGNED ΔB deviations from quiet-day H-component baseline, with negative=Sq suppression. applies_to enumerates 8 claim/criterion IDs (B1-M-001, B2-M-001, B2-M-002, OBS-D-02, OBS-D-04, OBS-S-02, OBS-O-01, OBS-O-03). prior_rev_inconsistency_note explicitly flags the rev1 mismatch and its resolution. B1-M-001 amplitude_range_nT_signed = [-25, -5] (signed, hard interval). B1-M-001 amplitude_caveats[0] explicitly says 'The "commonly" qualifier from rev1 is dropped' — closing rev1 P1-AMP-FUZZ as a side benefit.
ISS-1803_SIGN_CONVENTION_UNIFIED.verification: I cross-checked applies_to coverage against the bucket criteria. OBS-D-02 uses signed convention ('|ΔB_observed_signed - ΔB_dome_central_signed|'). OBS-D-04 references signed range [-25, -5]. OBS-S-02 says 'peak SIGNED ΔB at any high-obscuration station is in [-1, +1] nT'. OBS-O-01 magnetic clause says 'observed signed peak ΔB is positive (sign-flip from both models negative convention) OR < -50 nT'. OBS-O-03 references signed ΔB throughout. All 8 claim/criterion IDs in applies_to are covered.
ISS-1803_SIGN_CONVENTION_UNIFIED.one_residual_note: B2-M-001 has both amplitude_range_nT (legacy field, [-12.83, -5.04]) AND amplitude_range_nT_signed (rev2 explicit, [-12.83, -5.04]) with identical values. The duplication is harmless and the explicit-signed field is the authoritative one (per sign_convention applies_to). Could collapse to one field in a future revision but not worth a blocker.
ISS-1803_SIGN_CONVENTION_UNIFIED.status: CLOSED — sign convention is unified at the file level, all eight claim/criterion IDs are explicitly covered.

ISS-1804_OBS_O_03_CANDIDATE_STATIONS.issue_origin: Rev1 P1-OBS-O-03 — OBS-O-03 referenced 'any non-dome-cited high-obscuration INTERMAGNET station' without pre-specifying the candidate set. Post-event cherry-picking attack.
ISS-1804_OBS_O_03_CANDIDATE_STATIONS.rev2_change: Top-level candidate_non_dome_intermagnet_stations block added under proposed_files[0].contents with 5 stations (MAB Manhay 50.30N 5.68E; DOU Dourbes 50.10N 4.60E; WNG Wingst 53.74N 9.07E; SUA Surlari 44.68N 26.25E; BEL Belsk 51.84N 20.79E). selection_criteria_pre_registered describes the inclusion rule (European mid-latitude INTERMAGNET, NOT in dome's 9, NASA-derived obscuration > 30%, set size frozen at 5). set_locking_note commits to OTS-anchoring fixing the set; any post-anchor revision lives in a separate dated addendum.
ISS-1804_OBS_O_03_CANDIDATE_STATIONS.verification: OBS-O-03 in B3-F-OURS now reads 'no signed ΔB dip greater than -2 nT (signed) is observed at ANY of the 5 pre-registered candidate non-dome-cited high-obscuration stations (MAB, DOU, WNG, SUA, BEL)'. OBS-S-02 in B3-F-STDPHYS extends to 'all 5 candidate non-dome-cited stations'. post_event_protocol step 1 explicitly retrieves data for all 14 stations (9 dome + 5 candidate). The candidate set is woven into 3 separate criteria — no post-event flexibility remains.
ISS-1804_OBS_O_03_CANDIDATE_STATIONS.one_residual_note: The selection_criteria_pre_registered uses 'NASA-Besselian-element-derived obscuration > 30%'. This is the threshold for inclusion in the candidate set — a hostile reader can challenge the 30% choice (vs 25% or 50%). However, the SET ITSELF is what's anchored, not the threshold mechanism, and the 5 specific IAGA codes are concrete. Acceptable.
ISS-1804_OBS_O_03_CANDIDATE_STATIONS.status: CLOSED — candidate set is enumerated, OTS-locked by file inclusion, woven into all relevant criteria.

ISS-1805_OBS_D_02_QUORUM.issue_origin: Rev1 P3-OBS-D-02 — quorum 'a majority of stations' was ambiguous (5 of 9? 6 of 9?). Combined with the noise-floor ambiguity (P1-NF), made the criterion unfalsifiable.
ISS-1805_OBS_D_02_QUORUM.rev2_change: OBS-D-02 in B3-F-DOME now reads 'Magnetic field at the 9 dome-cited stations during the eclipse window deviates from ΔB = -10.9 × coverage × FSF (BOU baseline, signed convention) by more than the noise_floor_pre_commitment threshold of ±2 nT at >= 5 of the 9 dome-cited stations under Kp < 2 conditions. Specifically: the dome formula is FALSIFIED if |ΔB_observed_signed - ΔB_dome_central_signed| > 2 nT for at least 5 of the 9 stations during the eclipse window.' The quorum is integer 5; the threshold is integer 2 nT; the Kp condition is integer < 2. All three numeric pre-commitments are explicit. Parenthetical notes the rev1 wording was replaced.
ISS-1805_OBS_D_02_QUORUM.verification: Counted: '>= 5 of the 9' is mentioned twice (criterion lead-in + 'at least 5 of the 9' restatement). The numeric 2 nT comes from noise_floor_pre_commitment.threshold_nT. The Kp < 2 is consistent with B1-M-001 amplitude_caveats (quiet conditions threshold).
ISS-1805_OBS_D_02_QUORUM.one_residual_note: The criterion uses '>= 5 of the 9 dome-cited stations' (clean majority of 9). With 5/9, the falsification fires at slightly more than half. A defender could argue 6/9 or 7/9 for a stronger falsification claim; analyst chose the bare-majority threshold. Reasonable design choice — strictly more falsifiable, which favors symmetric-falsification rigor.
ISS-1805_OBS_D_02_QUORUM.status: CLOSED — quorum is integer-pre-committed, noise-floor is integer-pre-committed, criterion is mechanically decidable.

ISS-1806_OBS_D_03_TRACKS_OPERATIONALIZED.issue_origin: Rev1 P3-OBS-D-03 — 'tracks NASA per-station obscuration' was qualitative; no test, no significance level. Post-event subjective.
ISS-1806_OBS_D_03_TRACKS_OPERATIONALIZED.rev2_change: OBS-D-03 in B3-F-DOME now reads 'Compute Pearson r1 = correlation between observed peak |ΔB_signed| (after baseline subtraction per pre-registered protocol in post_event_protocol step 2) and NASA-Besselian-element-derived per-station obscuration percentage across the 9 dome-cited stations. Compute Pearson r2 = correlation between observed peak |ΔB_signed| and dome-published "coverage" values across the same 9 stations. The dome formula is FALSIFIED if r1 > r2 AND the difference (r1 - r2) is statistically significant at p < 0.05 under a paired Steiger Z-test on dependent correlations (or equivalent paired-bootstrap test on 9-station data).'
ISS-1806_OBS_D_03_TRACKS_OPERATIONALIZED.verification: Three operational components: (a) two specific Pearson coefficients computed against named external sources (NASA Besselian-element obscuration; dome's published coverage table); (b) directional comparison r1>r2; (c) paired-significance test with explicit method (Steiger Z) and explicit p-threshold (0.05) — plus an equivalent paired-bootstrap fallback. Cross-references post_event_protocol step 2 PRE-REGISTERED PREPROCESSING (H-component, 1-min cadence, mean of 5 quietest Kp≤1 days in preceding 30 days, peak in ±60 min window). Decision rule is mechanically reproducible.
ISS-1806_OBS_D_03_TRACKS_OPERATIONALIZED.one_residual_note: Steiger Z-test on dependent correlations with N=9 is statistically marginal — degrees of freedom are small and the test has limited power. The 'or equivalent paired-bootstrap test' fallback is a reasonable hedge, but a hostile reader could argue we should have specified ONE method to avoid post-event picking. However, both methods are recognized for paired correlations on small N, and the file commits to whichever is run; the result must be reported with method noted. Marginal — borderline minor.
ISS-1806_OBS_D_03_TRACKS_OPERATIONALIZED.status: CLOSED — qualitative 'tracks' replaced by mechanical Pearson-r comparison with explicit significance threshold.

rev1_non_blocker_findings_status.P1-AMP-FUZZ: ADDRESSED — B1-M-001 amplitude_caveats[0] explicitly drops 'commonly' and treats [-25, -5] nT as a hard signed interval.
rev1_non_blocker_findings_status.P1-DERIV-PROSE: PARTIALLY ADDRESSED — B2-G-001 derivation prose still reads '12.00 - 14.32 × 2,534 / 3,199 = 0.658 km' which the rev1 review noted is exact-arithmetic-correct from full-precision inputs but cosmetically inconsistent with the displayed rounded intermediate (12.00 - 11.343 = 0.657, not 0.658). The frozen-file value 0.658 km / 1.317 km diameter is unchanged. This is a cosmetic rounding nit — keep moving.
rev1_non_blocker_findings_status.P1-TOL: ADDRESSED — B2-M-001 amplitude_caveats explicitly invokes the noise_floor_pre_commitment ±2 nT threshold as the falsification tolerance for per-station match.
rev1_non_blocker_findings_status.P1-METH-PRE: ADDRESSED — post_event_protocol step 2 PRE-REGISTERED PREPROCESSING freezes H-component, 1-minute cadence, baseline = mean of 5 quietest Kp≤1 days in preceding 30 days, peak = max |signed-deviation| in ±60 min window centered on local maximum coverage. Explicit pre-registration.
rev1_non_blocker_findings_status.P3-OBS-O-01-VACUOUS: ADDRESSED — OBS-O-01 in B3-F-OURS now explicitly says SYMBOLIC ON GEOMETRIC BUCKET (logically empty, preserved for symmetry) and ON THE MAGNETIC BUCKET only fires for sign-flip OR <-50 nT (cleanly outside both ranges). Overlap zone 5-13 nT magnitude is explicitly NON-DISCRIMINATING and does NOT trigger retraction.
rev1_non_blocker_findings_status.P3-PROJ-CONV: ADDRESSED — B2-G-004 has a new projection_convention block under it: 'Azimuthal-equidistant from North Pole (AE-from-N), R_disc / 90° latitude (charity convention)' with rationale and reference to §4.2.7a footnote at SHA cd8af4b.
rev1_non_blocker_findings_status.P3-OBSCURATION-DERIVE: PARTIALLY ADDRESSED — B1-G-005 still commits Hartland 88% as the only NASA-anchored per-station obscuration; the derivation_notes field was not added. note_for_other_stations explicitly defers per-station obscuration for the other 8 stations to NASA local-circumstances at evaluation time (methodology committed, values not). Reproducible at evaluation but a defender can still argue post-hoc cherry-picking on which stations to evaluate. Acceptable since the methodology is frozen and Besselian-element calculations are deterministic.
rev1_non_blocker_findings_status.P3-V51-FROZEN: ADDRESSED — post_event_protocol.dome_version_drift_policy commits explicitly: V51.x ≠ V51.1 between freeze and event, frozen file's bucket-2 stays as the V51.1-at-pinned-SHA commitment, dome's V51.x predictions noted separately in evaluation but not the test of record.
rev1_non_blocker_findings_status.P3-SUMMARY-COVERAGE: ADDRESSED — registered_predictions_summary table now includes B1-G-003 and B1-G-004 explicitly labeled '(input)', clarifying they are inputs to B2-G-001/002 not standalone predictions.

new_attack_surface_introduced_by_rev2.summary: Rev2 introduces 4 small lints (1 minor, 3 cosmetic). None rise to MODERATE. None are blockers.

findings.description: OBS-O-03 in B3-F-OURS uses the phrase 'no signed ΔB dip greater than -2 nT (signed) is observed at ANY of the 5 pre-registered candidate non-dome-cited high-obscuration stations'. The phrase 'dip greater than -2 nT (signed)' is mathematically unambiguous (signed value > -2 nT means closer to zero or positive, i.e., NO significant negative dip — which is what's intended) but verbally awkward. A casual reader might parse 'greater dip' as 'larger-magnitude dip' (i.e., more negative than -2 nT) and arrive at the wrong interpretation.
findings.recommendation: Acceptable as-is — the parenthetical '(signed)' anchors the math, and the criterion is internally consistent. If polished further (post-anchor errata file), consider rewording to 'observed signed ΔB > -2 nT (i.e., no significant negative deviation beyond the noise floor)'. NOT a blocker — file is OTS-anchorable as-is.

findings.description: OBS-D-04 in B3-F-DOME reads 'At least one of the 9 dome-cited stations is observed to be inside the standard-physics path of partial eclipse (i.e., NASA-Besselian-element-derived obscuration > 0%) AND records a SIGNED ΔB deviation in the Chapman-Sq predicted range [-25, -5] nT for that obscuration during quiet conditions (Kp < 2).' The first clause is trivially true on 2026-08-12 — all 9 European-Iberian-Atlantic stations sit inside NASA's partial-eclipse footprint. The discriminator is the second clause; the conjunction with the trivial-true first clause adds no information. Hostile reader can argue we padded the criterion.
findings.recommendation: Drop the trivial-true first clause or rephrase as 'The 9 dome-cited stations are inside NASA's partial-eclipse footprint AND at least one records...'. NOT a blocker — the criterion remains mechanically decidable and the second clause carries the discriminating power. A post-anchor errata file could tighten the wording.

findings.description: OBS-D-04 references 'Chapman-Sq predicted range [-25, -5] nT' which per B1-M-001 is the range AT HIGH-COVERAGE STATIONS specifically. A low-coverage station in the dome's list (Niemegk at 40% coverage, Lerwick at 42%) might legitimately fall outside [-25, -5] under Chapman-Sq without falsifying anything. OBS-D-04 says 'AT LEAST ONE' station — so the existence of even one high-coverage station (Ebro 95%, Coimbra 92%, San Pablo 90%, Hartland 80%) landing in [-25, -5] satisfies the criterion. The criterion is therefore weak but correct.
findings.recommendation: Acceptable as-is. A future tightening could restrict to 'at least one of the four high-coverage stations (Ebro 95%, Coimbra 92%, San Pablo 90%, Hartland 80%)'. NOT a blocker — this is a sensitivity nit, not a logic flaw.

findings.description: B2-M-001 has two amplitude-range fields with identical values: amplitude_range_nT = [-12.83, -5.04] AND amplitude_range_nT_signed = [-12.83, -5.04]. The first is the rev1 legacy field; the second is the rev2 sign-convention-explicit field. Both carry the same data. Harmless duplication but a hostile reader could ask which is authoritative.
findings.recommendation: Acceptable as-is. The sign_convention.applies_to block names B2-M-001 explicitly, so amplitude_range_nT_signed is the authoritative field by file-level convention. If polished, drop amplitude_range_nT in a future revision. NOT a blocker.

pass_1_absolute.summary: Rev2 Pass-1 spot check: sources, units, derivability, falsifiability, symmetry. No CRITICAL findings. Sign convention is now uniform, hard intervals are committed, falsification thresholds are integer-pre-committed. Three previously-MODERATE findings (P1-NF, P1-SIGN, P1-OBS-O-03) all CLOSED.
findings_carried_forward_minor: P3-OBSCURATION-DERIVE (Hartland 88% NASA-anchored only, others deferred to evaluation time) — not a logic flaw given methodology pre-registration.
P1-DERIV-PROSE (B2-G-001 displayed-intermediate rounding cosmetic) — within ±0.03 km confidence bound, file value 1.317 km unchanged.

pass_2_consistency.summary: Rev2 Pass-2: internal numerical consistency, cross-bucket coherence. P2-RP CLOSED (R_p=42.4 km uniform, all 9 multipliers reproduce within 0.05 rounding).
spot_checks_run: B2-G-002 declared R_p × all 9 station distances = stated multipliers (verified arithmetically).
Sign convention applies_to coverage = enumerated 8 IDs + cross-checked against bucket criteria — all 8 use signed convention in their text.
B1-M-001 [-25, -5] nT range AND B2-M-001 [-12.83, -5.04] nT range overlap in the [-12.83, -5.04] nT zone (both buckets correctly note this is the non-discriminating overlap).

pass_3_trap.summary: Rev2 Pass-3: trap-detection — would a defender exploit any phrasing? Three previously-MODERATE traps (P3-OBS-D-02 quorum, P3-OBS-D-03 'tracks', P3-OBS-O-01 vacuity) all CLOSED. Four new minor lints documented above (R2-OBS-O-03-PHRASING, R2-OBS-D-04-TRIVIAL-CLAUSE, R2-OBS-D-04-RANGE-COVERAGE, R2-AMP-RANGE-FIELD-DUP) — none are blockers.
pass_3_trap.defender_simulation_outcome: A hostile reader running a post-event attack on this rev2 frozen file would have very limited surface: (a) cherry-picking attack neutralized by 5-station candidate set + ≥5/9 quorum + Pearson-r operationalization; (b) noise-floor attack neutralized by ±2 nT pre-commitment + alternative procedural definition for record; (c) sign-flip attack neutralized by sign_convention.applies_to enumeration; (d) post-hoc preprocessing attack neutralized by post_event_protocol step 2 PRE-REGISTERED PREPROCESSING. The remaining minor lints (phrasing awkwardness, trivial-true clause, low-coverage-station sensitivity, field duplication) are cosmetic and do not weaken any specific decision rule.

ots_anchor_clearance.rationale: All 6 ISS-1801..1806 blockers from qid=294 are closed in rev2. 7 of 9 rev1 non-blocker findings are addressed; 2 are partially addressed but acceptable (P1-DERIV-PROSE cosmetic; P3-OBSCURATION-DERIVE methodology-frozen, values evaluation-time deterministic). 4 new minor lints introduced by rev2 — none rise to MODERATE. The frozen file is internally consistent, uses pre-registered numeric thresholds throughout, and has symmetric falsification with a defender-resistant operationalization.

pre_anchor_checklist.pinned_dome_sha_resolves: c3b9f56 — verified in qid=294 review via GitHub API; unchanged in rev2.
pre_anchor_checklist.pinned_review_sha_resolves: cd8af4b — verified in qid=294 review; unchanged in rev2.
pre_anchor_checklist.r_p_consistency: VERIFIED — 42.4 km uniform across B2-G-002, B2-G-004, B2-M-001 per_station, registered_predictions_summary.
pre_anchor_checklist.noise_floor_pre_commitment: VERIFIED — ±2 nT numeric, applies_to OBS-D-02 and B2-M-001 tolerance.
pre_anchor_checklist.sign_convention_applies_to_coverage: VERIFIED — 8 claim/criterion IDs explicitly listed and all use signed convention in their text.
pre_anchor_checklist.candidate_station_set_locked: VERIFIED — 5 IAGA codes (MAB, DOU, WNG, SUA, BEL) with WGS84 coords; set_locking_note commits to OTS-anchor freezing.
pre_anchor_checklist.quorum_integer: VERIFIED — '>= 5 of the 9' clean majority, mentioned twice in OBS-D-02.
pre_anchor_checklist.pearson_r_operationalized: VERIFIED — r1>r2 paired Steiger Z-test, p<0.05, fallback paired-bootstrap.
pre_anchor_checklist.preprocessing_pre_registered: VERIFIED — post_event_protocol step 2 fixes H-component, 1-min cadence, baseline = mean of 5 quietest Kp≤1 days in preceding 30 days, peak in ±60 min window.
pre_anchor_checklist.dome_version_drift_policy: VERIFIED — V51.x ≠ V51.1 between freeze and event handled explicitly.

ots_anchor_clearance.recommended_next_action: Decider may pop qid=295, mark EXP-283 cleared for OTS anchor, and proceed to anchor predictions/eclipse-2026-08-12-predictions.json before any external party can timestamp it. The 4 minor rev2 lints (R2-OBS-O-03-PHRASING, R2-OBS-D-04-TRIVIAL-CLAUSE, R2-OBS-D-04-RANGE-COVERAGE, R2-AMP-RANGE-FIELD-DUP) should NOT delay anchoring; if desired, they can be tracked as post-anchor errata candidates and addressed in a separate dated addendum file (per the file's own immutability_notice).

the_other_side: An adversarial dome defender presented with this rev2 file would have minimal attack surface on the operational specifics. The strongest residual line of attack is methodological-philosophical, not technical: (a) the ±2 nT station-uniform noise floor is charitable to the dome (uniform is easier than per-station); a sophisticated defender could argue that station-specific noise floors would have rejected more dome predictions. The file documents the choice and its alternative, so the rebuttal is 'we chose the more-charitable test'. (b) The Steiger Z-test on N=9 has limited statistical power; a defender could argue that even a true r1 > r2 might fail to reach p<0.05 due to sample size, and we'd be required to call it 'not falsified'. The bootstrap fallback is a hedge but doesn't fully neutralize the small-N concern. (c) The 'mutual corroboration → retract' clause OBS-O-01 is intentionally non-trivial only on the magnetic bucket; on the geometric bucket it is symbolically empty. A defender could argue the symmetric-falsification framing is therefore weaker than advertised. None of these objections invalidate the core trilemma test — they are dimensional arguments about test power, not logic-flaws in the predictions themselves.
honesty_notes: I did not re-verify the pinned dome SHA or pinned review SHA against GitHub via API in this run; the qid=294 review verified both and rev2 did not change either pin. I did re-confirm the file size (59435 bytes) matches what the queue item references and what the workspace mount shows. The arithmetic verification of all 9 R_p multipliers was independently recomputed from the per-station distances; this was the load-bearing closure check for ISS-1801.
